body.title: Ground Loop collapse design — T-collapse-daemons-to-ground-loop (map + chosen approach)
body.text:
# Ground Loop collapse — architecture map + design (for T-collapse-daemons-to-ground-loop)

Mapped 2026-07-08 (pre-compact). RESUME here post-compact: advisor-check the approach, then build.

## The Ground Loop is a plugin-host process supervisor (the ONE daemon structure)
- `devices/ground_loop/daemon.py` GroundLoop: runs `while True` in run_forever() (daemon.py:144-162, 15s poll). run_once() every tick does _scan_plugins() + _tick_daemons() + _supervisor.scan(). Errors caught so loop never dies. Master-off via flat-file HaltRegistry key 'ground_loop' (early-return).
- Starts via own __main__ (`python -m unseen_university.devices.ground_loop.daemon`), launched by groundloop_start.sh (systemd --user unit ground_loop.service, or nohup+PID fallback).
- TWO hosting mechanisms for other devices:
  1. PluginDaemon (plugin_daemon.py) — YAML `mode: daemon` descriptor; _tick_daemons()->plugin.tick()-> if subprocess dead, subprocess.Popen(start_cmd). Circuit breaker ~/.unseen_university/flags/<name>.breaker; after max_restarts fires on_failure (cc_recovery spawns `claude -p`). SEPARATE OS PROCESSES.
  2. RunmeSupervisor (supervisor.py) — discovers devices/*/groundloop/runme.py, imports each, runs its start() in a daemon THREAD inside the GL process (supervisor.py:109-119). Hot-reload on mtime; import/runtime error -> rename runme.py->runme.borkedpy + raise_alarm. IN-PROCESS THREADS.

## Correct shim-thread poll pattern (aider = canonical)
- BaseShim in unseen_university/shim.py (base start() is abstract — does NOT itself launch a thread; per-device).
- AiderShim.start() (devices/aider/shim.py:101-116): writes availability, connects bus, builds AiderWorkerListener, listener.start(). No subprocess/__main__.
- AiderWorkerListener (worker_listener.py): start()-> threading.Thread(target=_run, daemon=True); _run(): `while not stop.is_set(): _poll_once(); stop.wait(interval)`; stop()-> stop.set()+join. TARGET SHAPE.
- GL-native equivalent: devices/demo_daemon/groundloop/runme.py — module start()/stop() with _stop_event and `while not _stop_event.wait(INTERVAL):` (runme.py:41-63). RunmeSupervisor runs that start() in a GL thread.

## Granny today = standalone tmux process (VIOLATION)
- daemon.py run_loop() (granny/daemon.py:1189-1210): `while True: run_once(config, imap); _wait_for_work(60s)`. Emits dispatch-health every _HEALTH_EVERY_N=10 cycles. __main__ (1213-1234) writes ~/.granny/daemon.pid + SIGTERM/SIGINT + run_loop(). Per-cycle body = run_once(config, *, imap=None) (daemon.py:897).
- Launched by ./granny (repo root) -> tmux session 'granny' runs `python -m ...granny.daemon`. device.py:94 launch_command.
- GrannyShim (granny/shim.py) does NOT run the loop — only a watchdog thread (30s) that checks PID via self_test and, if dead+stale+pending, _restart_daemon() re-launches via tmux send-keys. NO granny.yaml, NO runme.py. Keeps persistent imap/bus handle + reloads config every cycle across cycles.

## Scraps today = GL-supervised SUBPROCESS (STILL a violation per Akien)
- daemon.py run_loop (scraps/daemon.py:56-70): `while True: run_once(); sleep(30)`. __main__ writes ~/.unseen_university/scraps/daemon.pid + signals. run_once ticks JOBS (StaleChatLogBackfiller).
- Launched by GL PluginDaemon via unseen_university/config/ground_loop/scraps.yaml (mode: daemon, start_cmd [.venv/bin/python3,-m,devices.scraps.daemon]). ScrapsShim is in-process no-op.
- KEY: scraps already runs 'under' the GL as a subprocess (option C) yet Akien named it a violation -> subprocess-with-own-run_loop is NOT acceptable. Target = in-process.

## DESIGN DECISION: Option B (runme in-process plugin) for BOTH granny + scraps
Rationale: hard rule 'no device runs its own daemon, PERIOD' + scraps-as-supervised-subprocess STILL called a violation => the target is IN-PROCESS threads under the single GL process, not a supervisor spawning daemon subprocesses. So:
- Add devices/granny/groundloop/runme.py and devices/scraps/groundloop/runme.py: module start()/stop() with _stop_event running `while not stop.wait(interval): run_once(...)`. RunmeSupervisor runs them as GL threads.
- DELETE from both daemon.py: run_loop(), __main__, PID file, signal handlers, _wait_for_work. KEEP run_once(), _load_config(), granny's _make_imap_if_bus_configured().
- Granny state ownership moves into runme.start(): the persistent bus (imap) handle + per-cycle config reload live in the runme module (build imap once in start(), pass to run_once each tick).
- RELOCATE granny dispatch-health emit into the runme loop (every N ticks) — the T-granny-dispatch-observability-gap WARN must survive.
- REMOVE the transitional configure_process_logging bridge (base.py) + granny __main__ logging — in-process under GL (a DiagnosticBase host) the canonical JSON sink is already installed. VERIFY the GL process installs the sink for thread logs (device_id binding); if runme threads need device_id stamping, that's the one piece to keep/adapt.
- GrannyShim: drop the tmux-relaunch watchdog (no external process to watch). Retire ./granny launcher. Update granny device.py:94 launch_command.
- Scraps: same — delete run_loop/__main__, add runme.py, retire scraps.yaml (PluginDaemon subprocess descriptor) in favor of the runme.

## Caveats to verify at build time
- Descriptor path drift: groundloop_start.sh:42 copies from $UU_ROOT/config/ground_loop/*.yaml but descriptors live at unseen_university/config/ground_loop/*.yaml; _PLUGIN_DIR = ~/.unseen_university/ground_loop/. Confirm what the running GL reads before relying on YAML vs runme discovery path (devices/*/groundloop/runme.py).
- RunmeSupervisor logging: confirm threads' stdlib logs reach the canonical granny/scraps JSON sink (device_id) — this is what replaces configure_process_logging.
- Scope boundary (ticket): collapse STRUCTURE only, do NOT change run_once dispatch/scrap LOGIC.

## Open question for advisor/Akien (post-compact, before building)
Confirm B (in-process runme thread) is the target vs C (GL-supervised subprocess). Reading of the hard rule says B. If B, this is a real restructure of granny's launch (tmux->GL thread) — advisor-check the bus-handle ownership move + the logging-sink replacement first.
